reviews.review_description: This game’s got such cozy vibes,  the tiny floating islands feel so chill, and the puzzles are low key challenging but in the best way,  keeps me hooked every time. would be even cooler with a leaderboard so we can flex our puzzle skills

The game is quite fun, especially if you enjoy the way mental challenges like Sudoku try to break your brain while you evaluate different numerical combinations. I know it sounds strange, but that’s the closest feeling I can compare it to. And while it may seem easy and simple at first, the truth is that as you progress, it becomes quite difficult — it stops being trivial and really makes you think seriously about the possible combinations. That said, it does have some things to improve in terms of interface and gameplay, but if you like mentally demanding games, I dare say this is a great recommendation. In terms of challenge and overall feeling, I’d give it 5 stars, even if it loses a bit in some technical aspects.

Before anything let me start with the game was paid for with my own cash but gamedevs did gift me a 50%off code but that being said would have gladly pay full price in a vr world so fast pace it's nice to have a relaxing sit down puzzle game great for new and experienced vr players alike trying for a video on it on my channel realchillgaming so look forward to that w.e that is lol till then do believe it's a different experience in this ever change we call vr and hope this helped laterz

reviews.review_title: Fun, relaxing switch off from the world
reviews.review_description:
Sometimes you just want a little bit of peace and switch off and this game is perfect for that. Fun, quirky and uses mixed reality well (haven’t tried full).

The level difficulty does ramp up to keep you interested and they have added leaderboards which is a nice touch. I did find the UI a little clunky at first but I’m relatively new to VR (so likely to be a me issues) and also conscious that the game is constantly being improved.

Life is not all about shooters and epic titles, if you want a bit of zen, this game is great and the developers are constantly improving it so looking forward to keep coming back.

Lately I've been into playing lots of puzzle games, and GridTsugi scratches that hitch in VR. Your goal is to put in bridges between islands, but the island can only support the amount of bridges that is indicated on top.

You can only generate a single or a side by side bridge, which counts as 2. It's really fun and a great brain teaser. You have to go through a process of elimination to clear off bridges where you're 100 percent certain of the pattern, and as you keep going, it becomes more clear on what you need to do on other islands.

As you keep going, you'll unlock more cosmetics for the level that you're in. I really like this neon-retro-wave like world while putting some synth music in the background.

And since we're close to Halloween, they even have a nice scary theme here to put you in the mood.

Even if you're done all levels, they are procedurally generated, so you can replay them with a completely different setup each time.

They also recently added a leaderboard, so if you're looking to compete to get to the top, that's an option.

GridTsugi is currently in early access and some nice updates are cooking soon. 
You can currently play in Mixed Reality mode, but there is a full VR adoption in development.
You can expect more levels, better water shaders, and they're also teasing a Cozy Update, which I'm curious about.
A very cool game. The puzzles are challenging and interesting. The developer did a great job and made a good game.

I played GridTsugi on a Quest 3 for about an hour, using both controllers and hand tracking. I used mixed reality; I didn’t find a full-VR option.

Gameplay and learning curve:
The core loop is classic bridges/Hashi. If you like logic puzzles, the grids are decent. For me it got repetitive fast. The tutorial felt a bit confusing because some movement and control choices are odd.

Controls and UX:
This was my biggest gripe. I shouldn’t have to press a button to bring up a sign just to grab and move the grid. Let me grab it directly, and let me resize it. I also had to keep pressing a button to move myself around, which never felt intuitive. The basic UX for going back, changing levels, or finding what I wanted wasn’t set up the way most games handle it.

Comfort and performance:
No nausea or fatigue. Seated play is comfortable once you figure out the controls. Frame rate was fine, no real hitches, and I didn’t notice heavy battery drain. MR anchoring was stable.

Audio/visual:
Numbers and lines are clear. Soundtrack is fine. MR works, but I don’t see why this needs to be MR; it came off a bit gimmicky.

Bugs:
I didn’t hit crashes or tracking issues.

Value:
Price is $8.99. If you’re a puzzle person who enjoys repeating the same ruleset as the difficulty ramps, this will keep you busy and the price fits. If you only dabble in puzzles, I’d pass.

Best and worst:
I liked that it felt finished at release, which is rare. I disliked the repetitive feel and the control scheme that made simple actions harder than they should be.

Verdict:
Good fit for dedicated puzzle fans. Not for me. ★★★☆☆

This relaxing  game glides into your space, as islands glide down to your space  for the new level and then glide off you for the when you’re finished. 

It has hand motion.(yay)  
it is so freeing and being able to use your hands. And the experience is quite intuitive and forgiving. it’s was fun throwing a stack of bridge  into the playfield with a pinch.    
There  are butterflies, greenery and water, and the music as well chosen.  

The gameplay was fast to pick up, I was confused for 3 minutes, pretty good for such a unique concept. I can say it  really fun and I just really good l difficulty curve as the game progresses. 

I’ve found it  relaxing and played way longer than I thought I would, clearly a 
game made with love.

reviews.review_title: If you like doing Sudoku's, get this!
reviews.review_description: While I don't think this game is perfect (still early access), I can say that I enjoyed my time with it. The concept is great, you must place simple or double bridges to connect islands, but it must add up to the number written on the islands. The thing is, the more you advance the harder it gets, but when you succeed it is very satisfying. I really wish you could resize the map though, but overall It's a  fun relaxing puzzle game.

Cute and comfy aesthetics and engaging puzzle gameplay makes Grid-Tsugi a great game to wind down from a busy day with. Mixed reality keeps you grounded but gives you something fun to solve. A great game.

GridTsugi is stragegy planning brilliance! It takes the calm satisfaction of moving bridges to connect islands and elevates it into an immersive VR world that I found engaging. The mechanics are simple to learn but devilishly clever, making every level feel like a satisfying “a-ha!” moment when you crack it.

Clean visuals, smooth controls, and perfectly paced difficulty make it both relaxing and addictive, the kind of game you pop into for “just one more puzzle” and suddenly an hour has vanished. Even if you have not a lot of room the controls are so good you can manipulate the playing area so easily, this is great to add to your Quest library!

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot for the feedback. so happy you enjoyed it!

reviews.review_title: I really enjoyed this puzzling type app
reviews.review_description: I really didn't expect to enjoy this app from what I saw in the description and presentation trailer, but boy, was I pleasantly surprised. I only completed 2 levels so far, but they were challenging and quite fun to solve, putting the correct amount of bridges connecting the little islands. I definitely would recommend this app.

developer_response.body: Really appreciate your feedback. so happy you enjoyed it!

reviews.review_description:
This is yet another one of those games that are easy to learn and hard to master! The rules are simple: Use 2 different types of bridges (single bridge or double bridge) to link all of the houses shown into one whole network. Each house has a number above it indicating exactly how many bridges must connect to it... No more, no less! It's quite a hard game but there's no time limit, so you can relax while you use logic and math to solve the puzzle!

I've played this in early access and there are definitely some rough spots in the presentation and the controls (I feel the controller grip button should be used to move the map), but I've had a ton of fun and I do feel this game has the potential to become a winner once it's officially released! ⭐🤩

This is a great addition to the world of Mixed Reality! 

Wonderful cozy little brain teasing/training game, it’s all extremely well executed and very polished. 

I love the fact that soon as you think you have it all Sussed out you are moving bridges because it’s just not quite right. 

It does not get boring and the next environments that get unlocked are awesome. 

Definitely a game that I will continue to enjoy returning to. 

Great job devs!

Building bridges is the name of the game
I played in mixed reality, it looks good and plays well
I love puzzle games so this hits the mark.
Joining the islands is the goal, and it’s very easy to get into and understand what needs to happen.
Fair play to the dev.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the feedback. so happy you liked it!

reviews.review_description: This is one of the best games on the quest, I like puzzle games and this one is a really good game that has almost everything perfect, I recommend it especially if you like puzzles games and want to use your brain and a great game for kids also

I recently tried GridTsugi and was pleasantly surprised. The game’s concept of connecting floating islands with woven bridges is both creative and relaxing. The graphics are colorful and charming, making the atmosphere very enjoyable.

What I like the most is how the puzzles start off simple but gradually become more complex, requiring both strategy and patience. It gives a real sense of accomplishment when you finally complete a tricky stage.

Since the app is still in early access, there are a few areas that could be improved, such as smoother tutorials or additional level variety. But overall, it already feels polished and fun.

Highly recommended for puzzle lovers who want a fresh, thoughtful challenge in a calm environment.

GridTsugi is a fun and refreshing Mixed Reality puzzle game that feels like a clever twist on Sudoku. Each island shows a number, and your task is to connect it with the correct number of bridges—simple to learn, but engaging to master.

The execution is solid, with smooth mechanics and charming, cute graphics that make solving puzzles even more enjoyable.

It’s a refreshing concept that fits perfectly in VR/MR, and a great addition to any puzzle fan’s library. Recommended.

reviews.review_title: Smart Design, Great Potential, and Lots
reviews.review_description:
This game has a lot of potential! The core gameplay is very engaging. it feels like solving Sudoku, requiring thoughtful strategy while still being enjoyable. You can clearly see the amount of effort and technical skill the developer has put into the system design, which not only adds challenge but also creates delightful surprises every time the islands are procedurally generated.

There are still some details that could be improved, such as the menu feeling a bit too close to the viewpoint, which can cause slight discomfort. However, compared to the overall experience, this is only a minor issue. The game already offers both challenge and fun, and I believe it will become even more polished with future updates. If you enjoy puzzle or logic-based games, I highly recommend giving it a try!

This reminds me a little of the idea behind “Mine Sweeper”
Instead of having to guess how many mines using numbers on flags, you use numbers on little houses to guess the amount of connecting bridges and in the right places.
It’s a cute looking game, with relaxing music… but th gameplay can get tricky/stressful.. which is what puzzlers are all about :)

Some levels you think “how am I going to work this one out?! Looks complex!” But once you start, the bridges fall into place and it becomes clear.

It’s a very nice and fun puzzler… something all the family could play as it’s not complex at all.

I like the piano playing mushroom lol

I played in MR.. not sure if there is an option to turn to VR only.

A decent puzzler I recommend… especially if you like Mine Sweeper style puzzlers

If you want a nice chilled out relaxing puzzle game then this is for you 

Take your time and place bridges 

Nice music, Pleasant graphics, pure fun

Honestly, I was surprised by how much fun this game turned out to be , it’s relaxing but also gets your brain working! The puzzles really make you think about how to connect the bridges and it feels so satisfying when you get it right. The visuals are beautiful and add so much charm to the gameplay. I even had my mom try it and she enjoyed it a lot!! Definitely recommend if you like relaxing yet engaging puzzle games.✨

GridTsugi is a wonderfully charming puzzle game that turns simple rules into endless fun. Reading the numbers on each island and carefully placing bridges to form one complete network feels intuitive at first, yet gradually grows more challenging in the most satisfying way.

The mixed reality experience of placing the board right in your own space adds an incredible sense of immersion. The cozy, hand-painted art style makes the atmosphere relaxing, and that “just one more puzzle” feeling easily keeps you playing for hours.

Solving each level brings a real sense of achievement, and finding a cleaner solution is even more rewarding. If you’re looking for a calming yet deeply engaging puzzle game, this is an absolute must-play! 🎉

I purchased this today at such a great price it'd have been silly not to. My first impression was that it had a similar feel to the Hidden Pictures game in the sense that it is on a floating platform. The platform can be manipulated. That's where the similarities end. So I read the
tutorial for a change. I've seen unfairly bad reviews because people can't be bothered to read the tutorials. On the table there are islands and each island has a number. You connect these islands with one or two bridges depending on the number on the island's cubes. Then you must connect all the islands correctly. At first I thought well this is easy but that was for the first 3 or 4 warm up games. Then it starts becoming more challenging as you progress. I would imagine that you need to finish each level before unlocking the next? It is an immersive game that gets you thinking which is good brain exercise. It is visually colourful and cheerful. You can play standing or sitting because, as I said, you can move the table and place where it would be comfortable for you.
I think I've covered everything and convinced you to part with your money. It cost me under 8€ which is an absolute bargain for such a great game that will give you hours of playtime. 
Say hi to the mushroom 🍄

developer_response.body: thanks a lot for the feedback. we will make the tutorial section better.

reviews.review_description: Grid-Tsugi is a fresh entry in the puzzle genre that has you connecting a specific number of bridges to each landform. I enjoyed the progression as it became increasingly challenging. The only thing I'd love to see added is Quest Achievements to hunt.

Grid Tsugi is a mixed reality bridge building game that is great for destressing. There is strategy involved in game play...however it feels really relaxing. 

It's fun with math and a great way to sneak math into your kids' gaming schedule :)
It does get a bit more complex as you get along further in the levels. 

I tested this in early access. All opinions are my own.

GridTsugi charms with its elegant simplicity: clear rules, soothing graphics, and a bridge mechanic that quickly becomes a challenging puzzle with infinite replayability. What I love about GridTsugi is its balance between relaxation and challenge. You start slowly, placing bridges between islands, avoiding intersections, but very quickly the puzzles require strategy and finesse. It's rewarding when everything lines up. The "mixed reality" aspect adds a magical touch: placing the grid in your room or on a table and watching the bridges float is immersive and very satisfying. Visually, the hand-painted style is easy on the eyes, the levels evolve nicely in difficulty, and keep you wanting to discover more.
The game is accessible: seated, standing, and room-scale modes, which makes GridTsugi ideal for anyone looking for a relaxing puzzle without the stress.
If you enjoy elegant puzzles and quiet time between more intense sessions, this title will be a valuable addition to your VR library.
GAME MECHANICS
The gameplay consists of placing bridges between islands according to the connection constraints (number of connected islands), without ever crossing the bridges. In MR, the grid remains anchored in your space, allowing for natural interaction and the testing of varied and fluid solutions.
Video review on my Youtube chanel GuideMetaQuest.
this game is so cool you have to buy this and its a good price really fun !!!

reviews.review_title: A very unique and satisfying puzzle
reviews.review_description: This is a great puzzle game and has quickly become one of my favorite puzzle games. The puzzles themselves are very satisfying to solve. The game can  be challenging while not being frustrating at all. It's not too complex but it is very fun.
Very good game !But there is still room for improvement but it is still early access I trust the Hungarian subtitles
Beautiful cozy but challenging puzzling game, absolutely recommended!
Yeah, it’s a very simple game, but highly addictive. I can’t wait to see how they expand this during EA

GridTsugi impresses as a mixed-reality puzzle for the Meta Quest with precise mechanics and high quality. The bridge-building puzzles between floating islands are logically structured, demanding concentration without unnecessary complexity – ideal for short sessions or longer puzzling. In passthrough mode, it integrates seamlessly into real space, enhancing immersion and making controls intuitive, even with hand tracking. 
The hand-drawn graphics run smoothly without lag, and the levels offer enough variants for replay value. I see potential for expansions, but it's already a highlight among cozy VR titles. The developers have delivered something technically clean that provides fun and stimulates the brain. Five stars for a game that keeps its promises. 👍

GridTsugi is a fantastic mix of relaxing atmosphere and clever puzzling that's rare in VR. The hand-painted islands look truly charming, and the option to play in mixed-reality mode is a real highlight – it feels super natural building bridges right on the coffee table. Even though I haven't completed all the levels yet, the puzzle design is just fun without ever getting frustrating. The intuitive controls and lovingly crafted visuals make you want to come back again and again. For anyone who enjoys calm but challenging VR games, GridTsugi is definitely a must-play. It creates a cozy, almost meditative vibe that's perfect for unwinding after a long day. Five stars because it gives me that special feel-good experience as a player while also engaging my brain.
This is a fun game! Great puzzle format, solid mechanics, lots of levels, 3 tiered unlockable themes. Very enjoyable, definitely worth checking out.

The music is peaceful and there are no metrics (time elapsed) until you complete a puzzle and see how you fared against others. I felt no pressure or frustration when working on a grid level. 

I use the word rogue like because there are very few context sensitive prompts such as where to grab the bridge pieces and reorientation the grid. Also when you have many bridges in place it’s a little hard to review your pieces which will cost you time if you are going for #1 on the leaderboard. It might be nice to have a hint option if the game realizes a lot of time has passed.
This makes game you think, which is good for learning by doing practical things in the game.
No option to turn off mix reality. I was playing on a Quest 2. Tutorials give you no instruction how to touch any bridges or what to do.
Very good game made by you, it was recommended by my friend blinkeroke

I love that its mixed reality and it makes you have to stop and think about the bridge placement. Its got a nice relaxing ambiance as well which provides that calm space in the mind. And being able to move the island makes it so easy to maneuver and place. The little loading figuer is absolutley adorable too! Definitely a unwind from a long day game or to just help give a focus point to let go of stress! Haven't played through all the levels but its been really enjoyable so far and cant wait to play more when my headset charges. :D good job to the devs and creators!
It makes you think, that's for sure.
Its one of those games you can play while the TV is on or while having a conversation with a friend.. I liked the mallow atmosphere and relaxed play style. Highly recommend!
Fun game which quickly gets more challenging as you progress. Also includes a leaderboard so you can try to compete for fastest puzzle solver if you choose to.

The colors and the concept is amazing and exactly what I am looking for in a 'cozy' game. The music in the game is relaxing and feels immersive.  The tutorial is fun and a good way to learn the game. Hoping they add animals or plants (like that cute mushroom character) or being able to pet them for an animation feel would bring so much more to the game and make it even cozier. I love the game keep up the good work and developments.

I'm really enjoying Grid Tsugi so far! It's such a simple game, and once you get the hang of the few components, it's pretty relaxing. I've found it's a great game to just hop on and play a single board when I want to unwind. I also appreciate how accessible it is; I had no problem playing it while sitting down, and I could even do most of the actions with just one hand. This makes it a great option for people with chronic illnesses or mobility problems.

​The game is super bright and colourful, which is a big part of its charm. However, I can see how the vibrant visuals might be a bit much for some people, especially those with migraines or photosensitivity. It's a relief that the game works well in mixed reality, which definitely helps with any motion sickness.

​While I'm having a lot of fun, there are a couple of things that would make the game even better:

​More colour options: The game could really benefit from some in-game options to manage the brightness and colours. This would make it much more comfortable for a wider range of players.

​Smoother controls: It would be great to have the option to move the board with the joysticks. It would make the gameplay feel a lot smoother and more intuitive.

​Overall, I'd give the game 4 out of 5 stars. If these quality-of-life changes were made, it would easily be a 5 out of 5 for me.
It’s a relaxing puzzle game with a very unique world
